title: Kevin Warsh’s Three Tasks: Shrink the Fed, Tame Inflation, Manage the President.
title_cn: 凯文·沃什的三项任务：缩减美联储、驯服通胀、管理总统。

paragraphs.cn: 美国东部时间2026年1月31日上午5:30

paragraphs.en: Kevin Warsh has spent much of the last 15 years criticizing the Federal Reserve for getting too big, mismanaging inflation and compromising its independence.
paragraphs.cn: 凯文·沃什在过去15年的大部分时间里都在批评美联储规模过大、通胀管理不善以及损害其独立性。

paragraphs.en: So as President Trump’s pick to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the central bank, what does he do about it? He faces three key tests. First, significantly shrink the Fed’s balance sheet without upsetting markets. Second, push inflation down to 2% and keep it there. Third, do this without meddling by Trump that compromises the Fed’s independence. All will be tougher than they look.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，作为特朗普总统挑选的接替杰罗姆·鲍威尔担任央行主席的人选，他将如何应对？他面临三项关键考验。首先，在不扰乱市场的情况下大幅缩减美联储的资产负债表。其次，将通胀率降至2%并保持在该水平。第三，在特朗普不干预、不损害美联储独立性的情况下完成这些任务。所有这些都将比看起来更艰难。

paragraphs.en: As a Fed governor from 2006 to 2011, Warsh worked with then-chair Ben Bernanke to manage the global financial crisis. He quit over Bernanke’s use of “quantitative easing,” the purchase of trillions of dollars of bonds, paid for by issuing reserves (electronic money) to banks. Between 2008 and 2022 the Fed’s assets grew from $900 billion to $9 trillion. They have since declined to $6.6 trillion.
paragraphs.cn: 作为2006年至2011年的美联储理事，沃什与时任主席本·伯南克合作管理了全球金融危机。他因伯南克使用“量化宽松”政策而辞职，该政策通过向银行发行准备金（电子货币）来购买数万亿美元的债券。2008年至2022年间，美联储的资产从9000亿美元增至9万亿美元。此后已降至6.6万亿美元。

paragraphs.en:

vocabulary.meaning_en: Indicate the faults of (someone or something) in a disapproving way.
vocabulary.example: Kevin Warsh has spent much of the last 15 years criticizing the Federal Reserve for getting too big, mismanaging inflation and compromising its independence.

vocabulary.meaning_en: Cause a place or person to be vulnerable or open to danger or harm. (In this context: undermining or weakening)
vocabulary.example: Kevin Warsh has spent much of the last 15 years criticizing the Federal Reserve for getting too big, mismanaging inflation and compromising its independence.

vocabulary.meaning_en: The fact or state of being independent.
vocabulary.example: Kevin Warsh has spent much of the last 15 years criticizing the Federal Reserve for getting too big, mismanaging inflation and compromising its independence.
vocabulary.example_cn: 凯文·沃什在过去15年的大部分时间里都在批评美联储规模过大、通胀管理不善以及损害其独立性。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Achieve the desired aim or result. (In this context: take over a position or role from someone)
vocabulary.example: So as President Trump’s pick to succeed Jerome Powell as chair of the central bank, what does he do about it?
vocabulary.example_cn: 那么，作为特朗普总统挑选的接替杰罗姆·鲍威尔担任央行主席的人选，他将如何应对？

vocabulary.meaning_en: A statement of the assets, liabilities, and capital of a business or other organization at a particular point in time, detailing the balance of income and expenditure over the preceding period.
vocabulary.example: First, significantly shrink the Fed’s balance sheet without upsetting markets.
vocabulary.example_cn: 首先，在不扰乱市场的情况下大幅缩减美联储的资产负债表。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Causing an unexpected and unwelcome event to happen. (In this context: causing disturbance or distress to)
vocabulary.example: First, significantly shrink the Fed’s balance sheet without upsetting markets.
vocabulary.example_cn: 首先，在不扰乱市场的情况下大幅缩减美联储的资产负债表。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The action of inflating something or the state of being inflated. (In economics: a general increase in prices and fall in the purchasing value of money)
vocabulary.example: Second, push inflation down to 2% and keep it there.
vocabulary.example_cn: 其次，将通胀率降至2%并保持在该水平。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Interfere in or busy oneself unduly with something that is not one's concern.
vocabulary.example: Third, do this without meddling by Trump that compromises the Fed’s independence.
vocabulary.example_cn: 第三，在特朗普不干预、不损害美联储独立性的情况下完成这些任务。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A monetary policy whereby a central bank buys predetermined amounts of government bonds or other financial assets in order to inject money directly into the economy.
vocabulary.example: He quit over Bernanke’s use of “quantitative easing,” the purchase of trillions of dollars of bonds, paid for by issuing reserves (electronic money) to banks.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他因伯南克使用“量化宽松”政策而辞职，该政策通过向银行发行准备金（电子货币）来购买数万亿美元的债券。
